claimText: 'Gaurav' is an indigenously developed Long-Range Glide Bomb developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO).
evidenceExcerpt: DRDO successfully conducts release trials of indigenously developed Long-Range Glide Bomb 'Gaurav'.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the developer and nature of the 'Gaurav' weapon system.
distractorHooks: Developed by HAL or a foreign entity like Israel or Russia
It is a ballistic missile or cruise missile instead of a glide bomb

claimText: The 'Gaurav' glide bomb utilizes a hybrid navigation scheme that combines data from the Indian Navigation System (INS) and the Global Positioning System (GPS).
evidenceExcerpt: Glide bomb after launch steer towards the target using highly accurate hybrid navigation scheme with a combination of Indian Navigation System (INS) and Global Positioning System (GPS) data.
examUse: Statement-based question on the navigation technology used in indigenous glide bombs.
distractorHooks: Uses only GPS without indigenous systems
Relies solely on active radar homing

claimText: The 'Gaurav' glide bomb belongs to the 1,000 kg weight class and has a strike range of 100 km.
evidenceExcerpt: Weight: 1,000 kg class. Range: 'Gaurav' achieved 100 km range with pinpoint accuracy.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question testing the operational range and payload class of the weapon.
distractorHooks: Weight class of 100 kg or 500 kg
